agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Areas with higher log nonprofit branch density are denser charity hubs, where organizations are larger and conduct more event-based fundraising, thus spending more on event expenses.
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Bielefeld (2000) discusses clustering effects and geographic agglomeration of non-profit operations.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A statistically significant positive correlation was found. An increase in log nonprofit branch density is associated with higher direct event expenses, indicating that local nonprofit density scales alongside the size and event-spending of localized organizations.
